description: Trigger discrimination for file/object storage + upload architecture (this skill) vs the personal-data lifecycle of file CONTENTS (pii-lifecycle-designer) and auditing existing storage RLS/bucket policies (rls-policy-auditor).

cases.prompt: Design our file uploads: direct-to-storage with scoped signed URLs, tenant-prefixed object keys, magic-byte content validation, malware scanning before serve, thumbnails as background jobs, and a retention rule per object class.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — upload flow, signed-URL scope, key derivation, content validation, scanning, derivatives, and retention are storage architecture. No consent/subject-access obligations and no audit of an EXISTING policy are requested.
cases.reason: The storage and upload architecture — where and how files live and move — is this skill. It hands policy verification to rls-policy-auditor and content privacy to pii-lifecycle-designer.

cases.prompt: The uploaded documents contain personal data. Design the lifecycle: lawful basis and consent, how we honor data-subject access and export, PII classification, and the deletion SLA when a user is erased.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — consent, subject access/export, and erasure SLAs are the personal-data lifecycle, independent of where the bytes are stored.
cases.reason: The privacy obligations on file CONTENTS are pii-lifecycle-designer. Storage design provides the deletion mechanism; it does not define the lawful basis, subject-access, or erasure SLA.

cases.prompt: We already have storage bucket policies and row-level rules for our object metadata. Audit them for holes — can any tenant read or write another tenant's objects, and are the policies deny-by-default?
cases.reason: Auditing EXISTING RLS/bucket policies for cross-tenant holes and deny-by-default posture is rls-policy-auditor. This skill DESIGNS the storage access model and hands verification of existing policies there.
